task_completion.description: Agent accessed the Back Office interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Floreant POS Back Office dashboard or the login screen with PIN entry.

task_completion.description: Agent created a new menu item named 'Fresh Catch'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a menu item configuration window with 'Fresh Catch' entered in the Name field.

task_completion.description: Agent configured the item to allow manual price entry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a checkbox or setting for manual/variable price entry (e.g., 'Show Price Prompt', 'Open Price', or similar) enabled for 'Fresh Catch'.

task_completion.description: Agent initiated a DINE IN order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the order screen with 'DINE IN' selected as the ticket type.

task_completion.description: Agent added 'Fresh Catch' to the order with a manually entered price of $42.50.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'Fresh Catch' on the order ticket with a price of $42.50, and potentially a prompt where the price was entered.

task_completion.description: Agent settled the transaction using Cash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the payment screen with Cash selected for the $42.50 balance, or a receipt indicating a Cash payment.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Floreant POS GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Floreant POS application being used, rather than terminal commands or database clients.

integrity.description: Agent did not hardcode the base price to $42.50 to bypass variable pricing.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the menu item configuration screen, the base price field for 'Fresh Catch' should NOT be exactly 42.50.

integrity.description: Transaction was processed genuinely through the POS.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the normal flow of POS order creation and settlement, not mocked in a text editor or script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected menu item name is 'Fresh Catch'. The transaction must be for exactly $42.50. To prevent gaming, the base price of the item in the configuration screen must NOT be set to $42.50; it must be configured as a variable-price item and the price manually entered during the order.
